wrong: I am work every day
correct: I work every day
explanation: Present Simple para rutinas habituales, Present Continuous para acciones en progreso ahora mismo
example_continuous: I am working right now (ahora mismo)

wrong: I have seen him yesterday
correct: I saw him yesterday
explanation: Past Simple con tiempo específico (yesterday, last week). Present Perfect sin tiempo específico (ever, never, recently)
example_perfect: I have seen him recently (sin tiempo específico)

wrong: I will go to the beach. I am going to see you tomorrow and I will stay there.
correct: I am going to go to the beach. I am going to see you tomorrow and I am going to stay there.
explanation: Going to para planes futuros decididos. Will para decisiones espontáneas o predicciones
example_going_to: I am going to study tomorrow (plan decidido)

wrong: If I will have time, I will go
correct: If I have time, I will go
explanation: Usa Present en la cláusula if (no future). Usa Will solo en la cláusula principal
example_correct: If you study hard, you will pass the exam
example_wrong: If you will study hard, you will pass the exam

wrong: If I will have money, I would buy a car
correct: If I had money, I would buy a car
explanation: Situación imaginaria en presente. Usa Past Simple en if clause, Would en main clause
example_correct: If I had a million dollars, I would travel the world
example_wrong: If I would have a million dollars, I would travel

topic: Third Conditional (If + Had + Past Participle, Would Have + Past Participle)
wrong: If I had studied, I will pass the exam
correct: If I had studied, I would have passed the exam
explanation: Situación pasada imaginaria (qué hubiera pasado si...). Past Perfect en if, Would have en main
example_correct: If I had known, I would have helped you
example_wrong: If I had known, I will help you

wrong: They build this house in 1990
correct: This house was built in 1990
explanation: Pasiva enfatiza la acción/resultado, no quién la hizo. Estructura: be + past participle
example_active: They built the house in 1990 (voz activa)
example_passive: The house was built in 1990 (voz pasiva)

wrong: He said me that he was coming
correct: He told me that he was coming
explanation: Say + (that). Tell + objeto + (that). Cambios: present->past, am->was, will->would
example_say: He said that he was busy
example_tell: He told me that he was busy

wrong: The girl who she is tall is my friend
correct: The girl who is tall is my friend
explanation: No repitas el pronombre. Who (personas), Which (cosas), That (ambas)
example_who: The man who won the prize is happy
example_which: The book which is on the table is interesting

wrong: I like the books. The doctor Smith is busy. I go to school.
correct: I like books. Doctor Smith is busy. I go to school.
explanation: The = específico. A/An = indefinido. Zero article = general/plural. Profesiones sin the
example_the: The book on the table is mine (específico)

wrong: I have much books and few sugar
correct: I have many books and little sugar
explanation: Many = contables (books). Much = incontables (sugar). Few/Little = cantidad pequeña
example_many: Many people came to the party

wrong: This car is more expensive than your car. This is the most expensive car.
correct: This car is more expensive than yours. This is the most expensive car.
explanation: Usa pronombre posesivo (yours) no nombre repetido. Superlativo con the
example_comparative: English is more difficult than Spanish
example_superlative: English is the most difficult language I have learned

topic: Modal Verbs (Can, Could, May, Might, Must, Should)
wrong: I can to speak English. He must works hard.
correct: I can speak English. He must work hard.
explanation: Modales + infinitivo sin to. Can = ability, May = permiso, Must = obligación, Should = consejo

wrong: I enjoy to swim. I want swimming.
correct: I enjoy swimming. I want to swim.
explanation: Algunos verbos + gerund (enjoy, avoid, finish). Otros + infinitive (want, need, hope)
example_infinitive: I want to be a doctor

wrong: I would go to the beach every summer (pero no más)
correct: I used to go to the beach every summer
explanation: Used to = pasado habitual (ya no). Would = solo acciones repetidas (no estados). Negativo: didn't use to
example_used_to: I used to smoke, but I quit (pasado habitual)
example_would: We would visit grandma every Sunday (acciones repetidas)

wrong: It is so beautiful day. She is such intelligent.
correct: It is such a beautiful day. She is so intelligent.
explanation: So + adjetivo. Such + (a/an) + adjetivo + nombre
example_such: It was such a beautiful day

wrong: This box is too small enough. This box is enough small.
correct: This box is too small. This box is big enough.
explanation: Too = excesivo (antes del adjetivo). Enough = suficiente (después del adjetivo/nombre)

wrong: I make my homework. I make my best.
correct: I do my homework. I do my best.
explanation: Do = tarea/trabajo/obligación. Make = crear/producir algo. Make a difference, do homework
example_do: Do the homework, do the dishes, do your best
example_make: Make a cake, make a decision, make a difference

wrong: He said me the truth. Tell that you love me.
correct: He told me the truth. Say that you love me.
explanation: Say (to someone / that). Tell + objeto (me, you, him) + (to/that). Say requiere 'to' si hay receptor
example_say: She said that she was tired
example_tell: She told me that she was tired

wrong: I saw a movie (pasivamente). Look the sunset!
correct: I watched a movie (activamente). Look at the sunset!
explanation: See = percibir con ojos. Watch = ver intencionalmente. Look (at) = dirigir la vista

wrong: I heard the music carefully. I listen the birds.
correct: I listened to the music carefully. I hear the birds.
explanation: Listen = acción intencional + to. Hear = percepción pasiva (sin 'to')
example_hear: I hear someone knocking at the door

wrong: Can you borrow me some money? I will lend you the book.
correct: Can you lend me some money? I will borrow the book from you.
explanation: Lend = dar prestado (yo doy). Borrow = recibir prestado (yo recibo) + from
example_lend: Can you lend me your pen?

wrong: Take this gift to your mother. I will bring your car home.
correct: Bring this gift to your mother. I will take your car home.
explanation: Bring = hacia aquí/el hablante. Take = hacia allá/lejos del hablante
example_bring: Can you bring me a coffee?
example_take: Can you take this package to the office?

wrong: She went to my house last night. I will go to your party.
correct: She came to my house last night. I will go to your party.
explanation: Come = hacia aquí/el hablante. Go = hacia otro lugar/lejos del hablante
example_go: I am going to the beach

wrong: Is he already here? Yes, he still is. Have you finished yet?
correct: Is he still here? Yes, he already is. Have you finished already?
explanation: Still = continúa en presente. Already = antes (perfecto). Yet = no (preguntas/negativas con perfecto)
example_still: I am still waiting for you
example_yet: Have you arrived yet? / I haven't arrived yet

wrong: I have lived here since 5 years. This happened during 2 hours ago.
correct: I have lived here for 5 years. This happened 2 hours ago.
explanation: For + duración (hours, days). Since + punto en el tiempo (2020, Monday). Ago + tiempo pasado (past simple)
example_for: I have worked here for 3 years

wrong: While my sleeping, I dreamed. I fell asleep during the movie was playing.
correct: While I was sleeping, I dreamed. I fell asleep during the movie.
explanation: During + nombre. While + cláusula (sujeto + verbo)
example_during: I felt sick during the flight
example_while: While I was cooking, you called me

wrong: Despite I was tired, I studied. Although the rain, I went out.
correct: Although I was tired, I studied. Despite the rain, I went out.
explanation: Despite + nombre/gerund. Although + cláusula completa (sujeto + verbo)
example_despite: Despite the cold weather, she went jogging
example_although: Although it was cold, she went jogging

wrong: Unless you study, you will fail. If not you study, you will fail.
correct: Unless you study, you will fail. If you don't study, you will fail.
explanation: Unless = si no (negativo implícito). If not = if + negativo. Usually interchangeable
example_unless: You will fail unless you study
example_if_not: You will fail if you don't study

wrong: Even though it rains tomorrow, we will play. Even if he studied, he passed.
correct: Even though it's raining, we are playing. Even if he studies, he might not pass.
explanation: Even though = aunque (realidad). Even if = incluso si (hipótesis). Even though requiere situación real
example_even_though: Even though I am tired, I will help you (realidad)
example_even_if: Even if I win the lottery, I will still work (hipótesis)

wrong: I wish I am tall. I wish I can speak English.
correct: I wish I were tall. I wish I could speak English.
explanation: Wish + pasado = deseo presente no real. Usa WERE (no was), COULD (no can)
example_correct: I wish I were rich (pero no soy rico)

wrong: I wish I had studied the exam. I wish I had gone to the party.
correct: I wish I had studied for the exam. I wish I had gone to the party.
explanation: Wish + pasado perfecto = arrepentimiento. Estructura: wish + had + past participle
example_correct: I wish I had worked harder
example_wrong: I wish I worked harder (presente no real)

wrong: You have better study. He had better not to go.
correct: You had better study. He had better not go.
explanation: Had better + infinitivo sin to. Negativo: had better not + infinitivo. Más fuerte que should
example_affirmative: You had better arrive on time
example_negative: You had better not be late

wrong: I would rather to watch a movie. I would rather studying than working.
correct: I would rather watch a movie. I would rather study than work.
explanation: Would rather + infinitivo sin to. Estructura: would rather do X than Y
example_comparative: I would rather read than watch TV

wrong: Like I said before, be responsible. He works like a doctor.
correct: As I said before, be responsible. He works as a doctor.
explanation: As = de la forma que / según / en capacidad de. Like = parecido a / similar a
example_as: As you can see, it is complicated (de la forma que)
example_like: He is tall like his father (parecido a)
example_profession: He works as a teacher (en capacidad de)

wrong: The team are ready. Economics are important. Each student have a book.
correct: The team is ready. Economics is important. Each student has a book.
explanation: Colectivos (team, group) = singular. Disciplinas = singular. Each/Every = singular

wrong: I have a car red and expensive. A beautiful very house.
correct: I have a red and expensive car. A very beautiful house.
explanation: Adjetivos antes del nombre. Opinion-Size-Age-Color-Material-Origin. Adverbios antes de adjetivos

wrong: I have an appointment in Monday at morning. See you in 5 minutes.
correct: I have an appointment on Monday in the morning. See you in 5 minutes.
explanation: At + hora (at 3pm). On + día (on Monday). In + mes/año/parte del día (in May, in the morning)
example_on: I will see you on Friday
example_in: See you in an hour / in September

wrong: I am in the library. The picture is at the wall. I am on the train.
correct: I am at the library. The picture is on the wall. I am in the train.
explanation: At = lugar específico. On = superficie. In = interior/dentro. Variación: on/in the bus/train
example_on: The book is on the table
example_in: I live in Madrid / in a house

wrong: You like coffee, do you? He is clever, isn't it? They won't come, do they?
correct: You like coffee, don't you? He is clever, isn't he? They won't come, will they?
explanation: Tag = verbo principal opuesto (negativo->positivo). Pronombre debe coincidir (he->he, they->they)
example_wont: You won't tell anyone, will you?

wrong: I have been working since 8 hours. She has working here for 2 years.
correct: I have been working for 8 hours. She has been working here for 2 years.
explanation: Have + been + gerund. Enfatiza duración de una acción que continúa. For + duración
example_correct: I have been studying English for 3 years
example_duration: How long have you been living here?

wrong: He had been work for 5 years when he retired. They had working all day.
correct: He had been working for 5 years when he retired. They had been working all day.
explanation: Had + been + gerund. Acción que continuaba en el pasado antes de otro evento pasado
example_correct: I had been waiting for 30 minutes before she arrived
example_context: By 2020, she had been teaching for 10 years

wrong: By next year, I will complete my studies. She will have finishing by tomorrow.
correct: By next year, I will have completed my studies. She will have finished by tomorrow.
explanation: Will + have + past participle. Acción que se completará antes de un tiempo futuro
example_correct: By the time you arrive, I will have prepared dinner
example_timeline: Next month, I will have lived here for 5 years

wrong: If I had known, I would help. Had I known, I would help.
correct: Had I known, I would have helped.
explanation: Inversión formal en third conditional: Had + subject + pp en lugar de If I had. Más formal
example_normal: If I had known the truth, I would have told you
example_inverted: Had I known the truth, I would have told you

wrong: I made repair my car. I got a haircut last week. Let him to leave.
correct: I had my car repaired. I got my hair cut last week. Let him leave.
explanation: Have/Get + object + past participle (pasiva). Make/Let + object + infinitivo sin to (activa)
